title: ON THE DEVELOPMENT OF A SSDNA – BASED BIOSENSOR FOR THE DETECTION OF R – (+) – KAVAIN
author: Ventura, Damian
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Piper methysticum (kava) is a plant indigenous to the Pacific Island region that contains bioactive metabolites known as kavalactones, with one of the most abundant being kavain. With there being little definitely known about their mode of action on the human body, more research is needed to further investigate how these natural products behave at the cellular level. Developing a highly specific and selective biosensor for these metabolites would facilitate any efforts investigating kava and kavalactones. This work sought to develop a ssDNA – based biosensor to detect the kavalactone R – (+) – kavain. Using a ssDNA – based library containing a N30 random region and R – (+) – kavain as the ligand, the isolation of aptamers was conducted through the systemic evolution of ligands by exponential enrichment (SELEX). Gel electrophoresis and Spectrophotometry were used to qualitatively and quantitatively monitor the ssDNA aptamers throughout the SELEX protocol. With a total of 33 rounds of SELEX conducted, the isolated aptamers were then cloned – resulting in 27 samples being sequenced in order to characterize the random regions. Primary analysis was conducted on valid aptamer sequences to identify any motifs. Secondary analysis was conducted to predict the secondary structures of the aptamers as well as the energies associated with them. Lastly, a fluorescent – based assay was conducted to investigate the binding affinity of the aptamers with R – (+) – kavain, with the ligand concentrations starting at 100 μM and dropping to 0 μM via serial dilution. Aptamers were isolated but were deemed not selective nor specific enough at the concentrations considered. With discussion on how future projects can be optimized, a DNA – based aptamer can be further developed for kavalactones, significantly facilitating laboratory studies regarding kava.
